mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the federal funds rate tightens financial conditions and reduces equity valuations through higher discount rates, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of observations during month-over-month rising FEDFUNDS periods over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 4.5% from a lower level, labor market deterioration triggers recessionary fears and risk-off flows that depress S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are non-negative in at least 50% of instances when UNRATE crosses above 4.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in consumer prices and the federal funds rate create a stagflationary impulse where real rates rise and margins compress, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL and FEDFUNDS both increase versus prior month
failure_condition: The interaction term between month-over-month CPIAUCSL changes and FEDFUNDS changes exhibits a Rank IC greater than -0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: In late-cycle expansions, month-over-month declines in retail sales signal weakening consumption demand that directly reduces corporate revenue forecasts and generates negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of observations when RSAFS declines month-over-month during late-cycle expansion periods.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in industrial production signals expanding manufacturing activity and earnings momentum, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of observations when INDPRO increases month-over-month.

mechanism_narrative: Monthly housing starts falling below 1100 thousand units signals a contraction in residential investment that spills over into broad economic weakness and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are non-negative in at least 50% of instances when HOUST is below 1100.

mechanism_narrative: Increases in payroll employment combined with decreases in the unemployment rate confirm labor market tightening that boosts wage income and consumer spending, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS increases and UNRATE decreases versus prior month
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to produce positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of triggered instances over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter increases in real GDP reflect accelerating aggregate demand that expands corporate revenue bases and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of observations when GDPC1 rises quarter-over-quarter.
